_comment: Phantom classes that already shipped — NOT legitimate, just not yet fixed.
Each is a class a ds-react wrapper emits that the DS never defined, so the
component renders unstyled in every consumer. Mostly wrapper/CSS name drift:
the CSS calls it '.ds-segmented', the wrapper writes 'ds-segmented-control';
nav.css defines no '__item' at all. The backward-compat law forbids renaming
published CSS classes, so the fix is always to correct the wrapper.
This list may only ever SHRINK: mount-all.test.tsx fails on any new entry,
freezing the debt at today's size while blocking new phantoms outright.
Kept as data, not as TS literals, so the ds-no-phantom-classes hook (which
guards source files) does not fire on the very list that tracks them.
